description: A cognitive psychology instructor needs an analysis of N-back task data located at ~/pebl/data/nback_data.csv, which includes columns for subject_id, nback_level, trial_num, is_target, response, and rt_ms. Calculate the signal detection sensitivity index (d-prime) for each of the three n-back levels for every participant. Apply the log-linear rule for extreme value corrections by adjusting Hit Rates (based on 20 targets) to a range of 0.5/20 to 19.5/20 and False Alarm Rates (based on 40 lures) to a range of 0.5/40 to 39.5/40. Calculate d-prime as Z(Hit Rate) - Z(False Alarm Rate) using the inverse cumulative normal distribution. Exclude 'button-masher' participants with an overall False Alarm Rate across all 180 trials greater than 0.80 from the group mean calculations. Save a JSON report to ~/pebl/analysis/nback_report.json containing a 'participants' list with individual IDs, level-specific d-prime values, and exclusion status/reason, as well as a 'group_mean_dprime' object with the mean d-prime for each level across valid participants.
